title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
slides: Arena made $100M ranking models. Who's next?
It started as a hobby comparing chatbots.
But enterprises now pay for its private leaderboards.
You can test your models against real users today.
Stop guessing—let user votes pick your AI.
thread_text:
1. $100M for rankings

A startup made 100 million dollars ranking AI models. That's not a valuation—it's revenue.

2. It was a hobby

A side project comparing chatbots turned into a business. Now enterprises pay for private leaderboards.

3. Test with real users

You don't need benchmarks anymore. Just feed your model to real users and let them vote.

4. User votes decide

Stop guessing which model is better. Let thousands of user votes pick your next AI.

5. Try it this week

Submit a model to Arena's platform today. Run one test against real users—see who wins.

instagram.caption:
An AI model ranking site just hit $100M in revenue—from subscriptions alone. 🏆

It started as a hobby comparing chatbots. Now enterprises pay for private leaderboards where real users vote on which model wins. 🤖

You don't need benchmarks anymore. Submit your model, run it against real people, and let thousands of votes pick your winner.

What's your next move: keep guessing or test your AI against live users this week?

carousel_slides.description: A startup made $100M in revenue just by ranking AI models. That's not valuation—it's real money.
It began as a side project comparing chatbots. Now enterprises pay for private leaderboards.
Forget benchmarks. Feed your model to real users and let their votes decide the winner.
